proposed_relations.description: Average bus speed on 34th Street corridor

evidence_refs.source_quote: – 33,000+ daily bus riders – 4.5 mph average bus speed – 90% of travelers on the corridor are walking or taking the bus

proposed_relations.description: Average bus speed metric from Transitway project presentation

evidence_refs.source_quote: 4.5 mph average bus speed

rationale: The metric '4.5 mph average bus speed' appears on p004 of the 34th Street Transitway CAC #3 slides as a key fact about the 34th Street corridor. It describes a condition on the 34th Street corridor and belongs to the 34th Street Transitway project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 4.5 mph average bus speed

quarantine.note: Two proposed relations. The corridor relation is supported by block p004_c0004 mentioning 'the corridor' and metric scope '34th Street corridor'. But the project relation fails: the cited block text never mentions 'Transitway' or any project name — only corridor facts. Co-occurrence in a Transitway CAC presentation slide is not a relation per rules.

evidence_refs.source_quote: – 90% of travelers on the corridor are walking or taking the bus

evidence_refs.source_quote: 90% of travelers on the corridor are walking or taking the bus

rationale: The metric '90% of travelers on the corridor are walking or taking the bus' is a key fact from p004 of the 34th Street Transitway CAC #3 presentation. It describes mode share on the 34th Street corridor.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 90% of travelers on the corridor are walking or taking the bus

quarantine.note: Project relation unsupported by cited block text. Block p004_c0004 lists corridor facts ('90% of travelers on the corridor') but never mentions the Transitway project. Co-occurrence in project slides is not a relation.

proposed_relations.description: Key curb access finding informing Transitway design

evidence_refs.source_quote: Certain curb activities must occur right in front of buildings, e.g. Access-A-Ride, oil deliveries

rationale: This claim about certain curb activities needing to occur in front of buildings appears on p010 as a key finding from the curb activities survey, informing Transitway design.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Certain curb activities must occur right in front of buildings, e.g. Access-A-Ride, oil deliveries

quarantine.note: Cited bullet from p010_c0002 is 'Certain curb activities must occur right in front of buildings, e.g. Access-A-Ride, oil deliveries' — this bullet does not mention Transitway. The third bullet in same block does mention Transitway, but the cited bullet does not. Co-occurrence across bullets in same block is not sufficient.
